title: Robinhood Caps Record 2025 with Q4 Revenue Surge, But Shares Fall on Investor Concerns
text: Analysis of Robinhood's Q4 2025 results and full year 2025 record performance, despite post-earnings stock decline. Full Year 2025 Records: Revenue of $4.5 billion represented all-time high. Diluted EPS of $2.05 set company record. Net deposits of $68 billion surpassed previous records. Q4 2025 Highlights: Record Q4 revenue of $1.28 billion, up 27% year-over-year. Earnings beat with $0.66 EPS vs $0.63 estimate. Transaction-based revenues up 15% to $776 million. Net interest revenues up 39% to $411 million. Gold Subscribers: Increased 58% year-over-year to 4.2 million, significant growth in recurring revenue stream. Market Reaction: Despite strong results and full-year records, shares declined 5.4% post-earnings. Investors focused on revenue shortfall of $70M versus expectations. This was driven by crypto market volatility and softer trading volumes than expected. Investor Concerns: Cryptocurrency market exposure creates earnings volatility. Trading volume cyclicality tied to market sentiment. Valuation concerns at elevated multiples. Analyst Commentary: Despite pullback, analyst consensus remained constructive. The miss was viewed as temporary given strong fundamentals and upcoming catalysts. Long-term growth trajectory remained intact.
